theory_architecture.name: Theory of Liberty — Religion — Iran
theory_architecture.author: Mohammadali Jannatkhahdoost
theory_architecture.description: Layered architecture showing how claims build from foundational axioms to terminal conclusions across six layers.

layer_0_primary_axioms.label: Layer 0: Primary Axioms (Foundations)
layer_0_primary_axioms.description: Propositions treated as necessarily true; the system rests on these.

nodes.text: Human free will exists and is universally presupposed.
A valid formal system must have finite and minimal axioms.
A valid system must be internally consistent (non-contradictory).
Liberty and property rights are prior normative axioms to any scientific conclusion.
Theory has primacy over data in understanding history and religion.

layer_1_direct_consequences.label: Layer 1: Direct Consequences of Primary Axioms
layer_1_direct_consequences.description: Claims derivable directly and tightly from Layer 0.

nodes.text: Liberty = individual property rights.
Free will is the axiom everyone is forced to presuppose.
Denial of free will is self-refuting.
Acceptance of free will is prerequisite for science.
Liberty and property rights are prior to scientific conclusions.
Property begins with the human body.
Tawhid: no servitude to anything other than God.
No compulsion in religion.

layer_2_intermediate_theories.label: Layer 2: Intermediate Theoretical Constructions
layer_2_intermediate_theories.description: Multi-step derivations connecting axioms to applied domains.

nodes.text: Owning the body is necessary for being free.
Deterministic government is incompatible with volitional liberty.
Tawhid guarantees liberty by eliminating servitude to other-than-God.
Eliminating Tawhid proliferates axioms and leads to totalitarianism.
Formal systems must have finite, minimal axioms.
Gödel: humans are not machines; they perceive truths no formal system can prove.
A consistent formal system is necessary for understanding true religion.
Liberty is unstable without resurrection (last-round problem).
Resurrection exists; it stabilizes liberty.
Prophethood is barrier against fraud and false messiahs.
Law is discovered, not enacted.
Institution ≠ organization; conflating them destroys liberty.
Envy is the root of socialism.
Central bank = earthly god; violates liberty.

layer_3_applied_political_conclusions.label: Layer 3: Applied Political Conclusions
layer_3_applied_political_conclusions.description: Conclusions about governance, economics, law, and institutions.

nodes.text: Democracy does not legitimize the choice of the devil.
True religion purified of mysticism is the most precise system for liberty's defense.
Austrian School is incomplete without theological grounding.
Hobbes is more honest than Locke and Rothbard but still inconsistent.
Taxation is a penalty for creativity.
State education reproduces slavery.
Family disruption paves the way for the state.
Suffolk Bank demonstrates viability of free banking.
Mudarabah is the natural contract under free banking.
The formal system of liberty is equivalent to religion.
Religion is the antithesis of the state.
Rule of Taslīṭ is the religious form of property rights.
Liberty → property rights → religion (master derivation).

layer_4_historical_civilizational_conclu.label: Layer 4: Historical and Civilizational Conclusions
layer_4_historical_civilizational_conclu.description: Conclusions about Iran, Islamic history, and civilizational patterns.

nodes.text: Iran is the civilizational continuum — land of proprietors.
Iran's crisis: distortion of religion + theft of history.
Shahnameh myths are the civilizational memory of liberty.
Religion is antithesis of the state.
Ghadir = transfer of wilayah, not political state.
Islam entered Iran as theory of transformation, not only sword.
Iranian nation defined by liberty and property rights.
Iran historically independent from Hobbesian Leviathan model.
Hegel, Marx, fascism, communism are recyclings of ancient mysticism.
If Shi'ism were consistent with socialism/fascism, Iran would already be finished.

layer_5_ai_and_terminal_conclusions.label: Layer 5: AI and Terminal Civilizational Conclusions
layer_5_ai_and_terminal_conclusions.description: The theory's final claims about AI alignment and the future of human civilization.

nodes.text: AI tests consistency: machines cannot tolerate contradiction.
True religion as CFS must be translatable to machines.
Theory of liberty is the only existing solution for AI ethics.
A civilization without consistent principles collapses.
Without Mahdism, the theory cannot be completed.
Iran, if it returns to its truth, becomes the land of liberty and faith and a model for human-machine civilization.

cross_cutting_themes.description: Mysticism (in any historical form) → separates humans from property → delivers to collective entity → totalitarianism.
The book's driving motivation: Iran and true religion are not enemies but two pillars of liberty. Their false opposition is manufactured by mysticism and state.
The age of AI demands consistent formal systems; the theory of liberty claims to be the only one that meets the requirement.
